name: Chicago Manual of Style 18th Edition
turabian_reference: Supplemented by Turabian's Manual for Writers of Research Papers, Theses, and Dissertations (9th ed.)

notes_bibliography.note: First note for each source includes full citation. Subsequent notes use shortened form.

author_date.in_text: Parenthetical citation: (Author Year) or (Author Year, page)

citation_systems.important: Always confirm with instructor or publisher which system to use

general_format.margins: no less than 1 inch on all sides
general_format.font: Readable font such as Times New Roman or Courier
general_format.line_spacing: double-spaced — EXCEPT for block quotations, notes, bibliography entries, table titles, and figure captions
general_format.spaces_after_punctuation: one space after periods and other punctuation

page_numbers.placement: header of first page of text
page_numbers.start_with: Arabic numeral 1 on first page of text

general_format.place_of_publication: No longer required in 18th edition unless needed for clarification (e.g. two cities with same name)

title_page.required: Optional — either title page OR title on first page of text

turabian_title_page.title_position: centered, one-third of the way down the page
turabian_title_page.title_format: ALL CAPS centered (Author-Date); or standard headline capitalization (NB)
turabian_title_page.subtitle: End title line with colon, place subtitle on line below
turabian_title_page.author_info: Name + class information + date — follow several lines later, also centered
turabian_title_page.spacing: double-space each line on title page

no_title_page.first_page: Include title at top of first page of text, centered

headings.note: CMOS does not prescribe a heading system — devise your own but maintain consistency
cmos_recommendations: Maintain consistency and parallel structure in headings and subheads
Use headline-style capitalization for all headings
Subheadings should begin on a new line
Subheadings can be distinguished by font size
Ensure each level of hierarchy is clear and consistent
Differentiate levels by type style (bold/italic) and placement (centered/flush left)
Use no more than three levels of hierarchy

turabian_five_levels.level_1: Centered, Boldface or Italic Type, Headline-style Capitalization
turabian_five_levels.level_3: Flush Left, Boldface or Italic Type, Headline-style Capitalization
turabian_five_levels.level_4: Flush left, roman type, sentence-style capitalization
turabian_five_levels.level_5: Run in at beginning of paragraph (no blank line after), boldface or italic type, sentence-style capitalization, terminal period

headings.capitalization_style: Headline-style — capitalize all major words; do not capitalize articles, prepositions, conjunctions unless first word

nb_system.note_placement_in_text: After all punctuation EXCEPT the dash; at end of clause or sentence
nb_system.shortened_note_format: Author surname, shortened title (if more than 4 words), page number
nb_system.ibid_usage: Use 'Ibid.' for immediately repeated source; include new page if different
nb_system.no_bibliography_rule: If no bibliography, repeat full citation at first use in each new chapter

author_date_system.clarifying_words_omitted: Do not include 'ed.' or 'trans.' in parenthetical citations

by_author_count.three_or_more_authors_notes: First author only + et al. in notes
by_author_count.four_to_ten_authors_bibliography: Write out all names in bibliography; first author + et al. in notes
by_author_count.more_than_six_authors_bibliography: List first three, followed by et al.
by_author_count.author_order: List in the order they appear in the source, not alphabetically

in_text_citations.no_author: Cite by title — use up to four keywords in shortened form for notes/parenthetical
in_text_citations.indirect_source: Use 'quoted in' — Chicago strongly discourages; use only when original is unavailable
in_text_citations.indirect_format_note: Ian Hacking... quoted in Manuel DeLanda... 2.
in_text_citations.secondary_source_in_bibliography: Include only the secondary source you actually consulted

footnotes_endnotes.note_number_in_text: Superscripted, after all punctuation except dash
footnotes_endnotes.note_number_in_note: Full-sized (not raised) followed by period — superscripting also acceptable

note_formatting.commentary_and_source: Place commentary after source documentation, separated by period
note_formatting.parenthetical_commentary: Separate documentation from brief commentary with semicolon
note_formatting.page_ranges: Do not repeat hundreds digit if unchanged: 233-47 not 233-247

shortened_notes.when_to_shorten_title: When title is more than four words

bibliography_references.label_format: centered, plain (no italics, bold, or quotes)

spacing.before_first_entry: two blank lines between label and first entry

bibliography_references.alphabetization: letter-by-letter, by first word of entry
bibliography_references.doi_preference: Provide DOIs instead of URLs whenever possible; if no DOI, provide URL
bibliography_references.access_dates: Do not use unless publication date is unavailable

author_rules.format: Last, First (first author inverted); subsequent authors First Last
author_rules.two_authors: Both listed: Last, First, and First Last.
author_rules.ad_notes_four_plus: First author et al. only in parenthetical

title_rules.longer_works: Italicize — books, journals, magazines, newspapers, websites (if similar to printed work), films, TV shows
title_rules.shorter_works: Quotation marks — articles, chapters, episodes, poems, essays, web page sections
title_rules.wikipedia: Roman (not italicized) — treated as website, not encyclopedia
title_rules.all_caps_headlines: Convert to headline-style upper and lower case in citation

book_two_authors.note: Firstname Lastname and Firstname Lastname, Title (Publisher, Year), page.
book_two_authors.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname, and Firstname Lastname. Title. Publisher, Year.

book_three_or_more.note: Firstname Lastname et al., Title (Publisher, Year), page.
book_three_or_more.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname, Firstname Lastname, and Firstname Lastname. Title. Publisher, Year. [up to 6; 7+ use first 3 + et al.]

translated_book.note: Firstname Lastname, Title, trans. Firstname Lastname (Publisher, Year), page.
translated_book.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. Title. Translated by Firstname Lastname. Publisher, Year.

edited_book.note: Firstname Lastname, ed., Title (Publisher, Year), page.
edited_book.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname, ed. Title. Publisher, Year.
edited_book.note_abbrev: Use ed./eds. in notes; spell out 'Edited by' in bibliography

chapter_in_edited_book.note: Firstname Lastname, "Chapter Title," in Book Title, ed. Firstname Lastname (Publisher, Year), pages.
chapter_in_edited_book.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Chapter Title." In Book Title, edited by Firstname Lastname, pages. Publisher, Year.
chapter_in_edited_book.page_range_note: Page range for cited chapter no longer required in bibliography (18th ed. change)

introduction_preface_afterword.note: Firstname Lastname, introduction to Title, ed. Firstname Lastname (Publisher, Year), page.
introduction_preface_afterword.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. Introduction to Title, page-range. Edited by Firstname Lastname. Publisher, Year.

journal_article.note: Firstname Lastname, "Article Title," Journal Name vol, no. issue (Year): page.
journal_article.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Article Title." Journal Name vol, no. issue (Year): first-last pages.
journal_article.separator_note: Commas separate major elements in notes
journal_article.separator_bibliography: Periods separate major elements in bibliography

journal_article_online.note: Firstname Lastname, "Article Title," Journal Name vol, no. issue (Year): page, doi:XXXXX.
journal_article_online.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Article Title." Journal Name vol, no. issue (Year): pages. doi:XXXXX.
journal_article_online.access_date: Not required for formally published sources; include only if required by discipline

magazine_article.note: Firstname Lastname, "Article Title," Magazine Name, Month Year, page.
magazine_article.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Article Title." Magazine Name, Month Year.

newspaper_article.note: Firstname Lastname, "Headline," Newspaper Name (City, State), Month Day, Year.
newspaper_article.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Headline." Newspaper Name (City, State), Month Day, Year.
newspaper_article.page_numbers: Usually omitted due to multiple editions
newspaper_article.local_papers: Add city name in parentheses for non-well-known papers

website.note: Firstname Lastname, "Page Title," Website Name, publication/revision date, URL.
website.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Page Title." Website Name. Publication/revision date. URL.
website.no_date: Use access date: Accessed Month Day, Year.

ebook.note: Same as print + media marker at end: Kindle, PDF, EPUB
ebook.bibliography: Same as print + DOI or URL at end

blog.note: Firstname Lastname, "Entry Title," Blog Name (blog), Month Day, Year, URL.
blog.blog_title_format: Italics; 'blog' in parentheses after title unless word is in title

podcast.note: Firstname Lastname, "Episode Title," date, in Podcast Name, produced by X, podcast, format, runtime, URL.
podcast.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Episode Title." Produced by X. Podcast Name. Date. Podcast, format, runtime. URL.

online_video.note: Firstname Lastname, "Video Title," Platform, date, video, runtime, URL.
online_video.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. "Video Title." Platform. Date. Video, runtime. URL.
online_video.medium: Use 'video' for streaming without clear file type

social_media.note: Firstname Lastname (ScreenName), "Post text (max 160 chars)", Platform, format, date, timestamp, URL.
social_media.private_content: Cite as personal communication, not social media post
social_media.screenshots: Advisable to keep in case post is deleted

ai_generated.bibliography: Developer. Response to "[prompt]." Tool. Date. URL.
ai_generated.example_note: Text generated by ChatGPT-5, OpenAI, November 7, 2025, [URL].
ai_generated.general_rule: Cite in notes only; include in bibliography only if discussed in depth

indirect_source.note: Author, Title (Publisher, Year), page, quoted in Author2, Title2 (Publisher2, Year2), page2.

self_published.note: Firstname Lastname, Title (pub. by author, Year), page.
self_published.bibliography: Lastname, Firstname. Title. Self-published, Platform, Year.

ad_formats_by_source.note: Author-Date system places year immediately after author name
ad_formats_by_source.journal_article: Lastname, Firstname. Year. "Article Title." Journal Name vol (issue): pages. doi:XXXXX.
ad_formats_by_source.website: Lastname, Firstname. Year. "Page Title." Website Name. URL.

short_quotations.format: Inline, enclosed in double quotation marks
short_quotations.citation_nb: Superscript footnote number after closing punctuation
short_quotations.citation_ad: Parenthetical after closing quotation mark before period

block_quotations.prose_threshold: 5 or more lines, OR more than 100 words
block_quotations.format: New line, indented using word processor indentation tool
block_quotations.quotation_marks: Do NOT use quotation marks for block quotes
block_quotations.citation_placement: After final punctuation of block quote
block_quotations.font_option: May use different or smaller font to offset from surrounding text

quotations.sic: Use [sic] to indicate errors in original

tables_figures.placement: As soon as possible after first referenced; after the paragraph describing them if necessary
tables_figures.reference_in_text: By number: 'in figure 3' NOT by location ('below')

tables.label: Table + Arabic numeral (e.g., Table 1)
tables.title: Without terminal period, on line after label
tables.credit_line: At bottom, in parentheses or different type; cite as parenthetical + full entry in Bibliography/References
tables.unnumbered_footnote: Use if data not acquired by author; introduce with 'Source(s):'

figures.label: Figure + Arabic numeral (e.g., Figure 2)
figures.caption: Directly after figure number, with or without terminal period
figures.credit_line: After caption, distinguished from caption by parentheses or different type
figures.reproduced_adapted: Acknowledge appropriately: 'photo by', 'data adapted from', 'map by'

web_source_titles.generic_pages: No quotation marks for generic titles like Help or About

general.et_al: et al. — used in notes for 3+ authors (NB) or 4+ authors (AD)
same source as immediately preceding note (NB only)

key_changes_from_17th_edition: Place of publication no longer required in bibliographic entries or notes (unless needed for clarification)
Page range for cited chapter in edited book no longer required in bibliography
Up to six authors in bibliography (was same in 17th, but multi-author note rules clarified)
AI-generated content citation guidance added (new in 18th)
Social media citations expanded and updated
key_differences_from_apa7: Two citation systems (NB and Author-Date) vs APA's single author-date system
NB uses footnotes/endnotes, not parenthetical citations
Bibliography vs References — similar but formatted differently
Place of publication not required in CMOS 18th (was standard in older styles)
Notes use First Last name order; bibliography uses Last, First for first author only
Commas separate note elements; periods separate bibliography elements
No prescribed heading level system (flexible vs APA's 5 rigid levels)
Up to 6 authors in bibliography (APA allows up to 20)
Primarily used in humanities, not social/behavioral sciences
key_differences_from_mla9: CMOS has two systems; MLA has one (author-page)
CMOS NB uses footnotes; MLA uses parenthetical author-page
CMOS bibliography entries use 'Edited by' spelled out; MLA uses 'edited by' lowercase
CMOS uses headline capitalization consistently; MLA uses title case for containers, sentence case for sources
CMOS omits place of publication; older MLA editions required it
CMOS has more prescriptive rules for different source types
Both used in humanities but CMOS more common in history, MLA in literature
